extraction.one_line_summary: COUSIN Jérôme won from a breakaway that survived despite a late crash disrupting the peloton's chase in the final 2km.
tactical_patterns: POLITT Nils bridged solo to breakaway in final 15km, demonstrating Katusha's willingness to commit without sprint leadout.
Quick-Step and Lotto-Soudal took over chase from Sky despite no protected sprinter, revealing control priorities over stage win.
Break survived final crash in peloton with just 25-second gap at 2km, suggesting chase hesitation after organizational disruption.

rider_signals.COUSIN Jérôme: positive: won stage and secured polka-dot jersey by targeting all climbs before main broadcast, showing strategic breakaway point accumulation
rider_signals.POLITT Nils: positive: bridged solo to break in final 15km without team support, second on stage despite late arrival
rider_signals.SÁNCHEZ Luis León: neutral: retained yellow with ALAPHILIPPE and SOLER within 30 seconds, leader group neutralized on medium mountain stage
rider_signals.QUINTANA Dayer: negative: mentioned disrupting chase in descent suggesting poor positioning or tactical confusion for team leadership role
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: ranked 28th on medium mountain stage, revealing clear limitation when any climbing required despite early week success
